description: Gravity dependency classification for all 67 WINs. Identifies which claims depend on gravitational physics that the dome model has no framework to produce. The dome has no gravitational mechanism — no derivation of why things fall, no mass model, no density gradient. The g(r) formula in monitor.py is WGS84 Somigliana with dome labels (confirmed EXP-025). This map shows that nearly half the dome's scoreboard relies on gravitational phenomena it cannot explain.
methodology: Each WIN classified by how its claimed measurement, observation, or prediction depends on gravitational physics. GRAVITY-DIRECT: the WIN explicitly involves gravitational acceleration, gravitational fields, or instruments calibrated against g. GRAVITY-INDIRECT: the WIN's observation depends on phenomena requiring gravity (atmospheric structure, orbital mechanics, tidal forces as secondary effects, seismic propagation). GRAVITY-INDEPENDENT: electromagnetic, optical, or geometric observations that do not require gravitational physics.

summary.note: Nearly half the dome's 67 WINs depend on gravitational physics at some level. The dome model has no gravity theory — every gravitational quantity it uses is borrowed from globe physics without acknowledgment.

items.dependency: Earth-ionosphere cavity structure requires gravity to hold the atmosphere in place. Without gravitational stratification, no cavity exists to resonate.
items.what_breaks: No gravity → no atmosphere → no ionosphere → no EM cavity → no resonance frequency to predict.

items.dependency: Same as WIN-001. Schumann resonance requires a gravity-supported atmospheric waveguide between surface and ionosphere.
items.what_breaks: The damping calculation assumes a stable cavity whose geometry depends on atmospheric scale height — a gravitational quantity (kT/mg).

items.dependency: Eclipse geometry requires the Moon's gravitational orbit around Earth. The lunar position, eclipse path, and shadow timing all derive from Keplerian orbital mechanics (GM/r²).
items.what_breaks: Without a gravitational orbit model, the dome cannot predict when or where eclipses occur — it must borrow globe ephemeris data. OPEN-003 concedes this.

items.dependency: Claims a specific gravity anomaly (μGal-level) during a solar eclipse, measured by gravimeters calibrated against g = GM/R².
items.what_breaks: The dome has no derivation of g, no mechanism for eclipse-gravity coupling, and no explanation for what gravimeters are measuring on a flat disc. The instrument's calibration chain assumes globe physics.

items.dependency: Derives κ from dividing a magnetic anomaly (nT) by a gravitational anomaly (μGal). The μGal measurement requires superconducting gravimeters calibrated against absolute g.
items.what_breaks: If the dome has no gravity theory, it cannot predict what a gravimeter should measure, making κ = ΔB/Δg undefined. E-PRED-C (0.0 μGal prediction) creates a division-by-zero contradiction with this WIN.

items.dependency: Claims superconducting gravimeter reads 0.0 μGal during eclipse. SG instruments measure variations in gravitational acceleration using a levitated niobium sphere — the entire measurement chain assumes Newtonian gravity.
items.what_breaks: Predicting '0.0 μGal' requires knowing what non-zero reading to expect — which requires a gravity theory the dome doesn't have.

items.dependency: Same as WIN-013. Chinese SG stations (Wuhan, Lhasa) measure gravity variations calibrated against absolute free-fall measurements.
items.what_breaks: The dome reads globe-calibrated instruments and claims the readings as dome evidence. The prediction (null gravity signal) cannot be derived from dome physics because no dome gravity physics exists.

items.dependency: Stellar aberration is caused by Earth's orbital velocity (~30 km/s), which derives from the gravitational orbit: v = √(GM/r). The dome's refractive model must reproduce the same 20.5" effect.
items.what_breaks: Without orbital mechanics, the dome cannot explain why aberration has a 1-year period or why its magnitude matches v/c for a gravitational orbit.

items.dependency: Stellar parallax measurements assume Earth's orbital baseline (2 AU), which is a gravitational orbit parameter. Gaia's microarcsecond parallaxes require precise knowledge of Earth's gravitational orbit.
items.what_breaks: The dome's 'firmament wobble' must reproduce the same distance-dependent angular shifts as a 2 AU gravitational orbit baseline — a coincidence that requires explanation.

items.dependency: The analemma's shape is determined by Earth's orbital eccentricity (e=0.0167) and axial tilt (23.44°) — both gravitational orbital parameters.
items.what_breaks: The dome's solar formula hardcodes 23.45° (globe obliquity). The 6.9-minute day length variation comes from the equation of time, which derives from elliptical orbital mechanics.

items.dependency: Same as WIN-018. The loop ratio encodes the relative contributions of eccentricity and obliquity — both gravitational parameters.
items.what_breaks: The ratio is computed from Fourier analysis of the equation of time, which is a gravitational orbit computation dressed in dome labels.

items.dependency: The 18.613-year nodal cycle is a gravitational three-body effect (Sun-Earth-Moon). The dome's 'gear mechanism' must reproduce the period exactly, which means it's calibrated to the gravitational value.
items.what_breaks: OPEN-003 concedes the dome has no lunar mechanics. The 18.6-year period is a borrowed gravitational parameter.

items.dependency: Earth's axial precession (25,772-year cycle) is caused by the gravitational torque of the Sun and Moon on Earth's equatorial bulge. The precession rate τ/I depends on GM of both bodies.
items.what_breaks: The dome adopts the observed precession rate without deriving it from dome geometry. On a flat disc, there is no equatorial bulge and no mechanism for gravitational torque from a local sun/moon.

items.dependency: The Roaring 40s are persistent westerly winds driven by pressure gradients in a gravity-supported atmosphere. Atmospheric circulation requires hydrostatic equilibrium (dp/dz = -ρg).
items.what_breaks: Without gravity holding the atmosphere, there are no pressure gradients, no Hadley/Ferrel cells, no westerly winds, and therefore no 'Roaring 40s' to correlate with anything.

items.dependency: Eclipse path prediction requires lunar orbital mechanics (gravitational). The 9-station selection depends on knowing where the shadow falls, which requires precise gravitational ephemeris.
items.what_breaks: The dome cannot independently predict eclipse paths (OPEN-003). Station selection and timing are borrowed from globe ephemeris.

items.dependency: The Schumann cavity requires an atmosphere held by gravity. Satellites (cited as counter-evidence) are in gravitational orbits.
items.what_breaks: The dome's EM cavity argument requires a stable atmosphere whose structure is gravitationally determined. Atmospheric scale height = kT/mg.

items.dependency: Same as WIN-001/002/029. Schumann resonance requires a gravity-supported atmospheric cavity.
items.what_breaks: Stability of 7.83 Hz requires stable cavity geometry, which requires stable atmospheric scale height — a gravitational quantity.

items.dependency: The 1-2 nT lunar magnetic signal is a tidal effect — ocean tidal currents (driven by lunar gravitational tides) generate magnetic fields via electromagnetic induction. The signal's M2 period matches the gravitational tidal constituent.
items.what_breaks: Without a gravitational mechanism for tides, the dome cannot explain why this signal has a lunar period. The dome's gear-mechanism moon has no mass and no tidal force derivation.

items.claim: Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry

items.dependency: M2 is the principal lunar semidiurnal tidal constituent. Its period (12.4206 hours) is derived from the Moon's gravitational pull on Earth's oceans. Tidal prediction requires gravitational theory.
items.what_breaks: The dome adopts M2's period but has no tidal force derivation. OPEN-003 concedes no lunar mechanics. The dome's local moon at 2,534 km produces one tidal spike, not the observed two-bulge semidiurnal pattern — a geometric, mass-independent failure.

items.dependency: S2 (solar semidiurnal, 12.0000 hours) is driven by the Sun's gravitational tidal force. The dome's local sun at 5,733 km has no mass model to generate tidal forces.
items.what_breaks: Solar tidal force = GM_sun × tidal_factor. The dome has no solar mass, no gravitational constant, and no mechanism for how a local light source creates ocean tides.

items.dependency: Hubble's law (v = H₀d) in standard cosmology arises from gravitational expansion of spacetime (Friedmann equations). Galaxy distances use gravitationally-calibrated standard candles (Cepheid period-luminosity, itself a pulsation driven by opacity-gravity cycling).
items.what_breaks: The dome reinterprets redshift as aetheric refraction (z = d/λ_A) but the distance measurements it cites (Cepheids, SBF) are calibrated using globe gravitational physics.

items.dependency: CMB temperature anisotropies originate from gravitational potential wells (Sachs-Wolfe effect) in the early universe. The Axis of Evil alignment is a statistical feature of these gravitationally-sourced fluctuations.
items.what_breaks: The dome has no cosmological model — no expansion, no photon-baryon plasma, no gravitational structure formation. It cannot derive what the CMB should look like, only point at anomalies.

items.dependency: K1 (lunisolar diurnal, 23.9345 hours) arises from the declination of both Sun and Moon — a gravitational tidal effect requiring orbital mechanics.
items.what_breaks: Same as WIN-045/046. The dome adopts the K1 period without deriving it from dome physics. No tidal force mechanism exists in the model.

items.dependency: O1 (principal lunar diurnal, 25.8193 hours) is the Moon's declination tide. Requires lunar mass and orbital parameters to derive.
items.what_breaks: The dome's gear-mechanism moon has a position but no mass. O1 requires mass-dependent tidal forces acting on a spherical ocean — not a flat disc.

items.dependency: N2 (larger lunar elliptic, 12.6583 hours) encodes the Moon's orbital eccentricity (e ≈ 0.0549). Its amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 is a direct gravitational observable.
items.what_breaks: The dome never specifies lunar eccentricity (OPEN-007 self-admission). N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 is a globe fingerprint — it encodes e ≈ 0.0549 which has no flat-disc derivation.

items.dependency: The Radial Acceleration Relation (RAR) IS a gravitational relation — it connects observed gravitational acceleration (g_obs) to baryonic gravitational acceleration (g_bar). Gravitational lensing is bending of light by gravitational fields.
items.what_breaks: The dome's 'aetheric density' formula (ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g†) is a placeholder with no derivation. It reproduces RAR by definition (algebraic tautology), not from dome physics. The dome has no framework for gravitational lensing — it requires curved spacetime or a metric theory.

items.dependency: El Gordo's collision velocity (2,500 km/s) and the 6.2σ ΛCDM tension are computed using gravitational dynamics — N-body simulations, virial masses, and weak lensing mass estimates all require gravitational physics.
items.what_breaks: The dome has no extragalactic gravitational model. It identifies a real ΛCDM tension but cannot compute cluster dynamics, collision velocities, or mass estimates on a 20,015 km disc.

items.dependency: Cepheid period-luminosity relation is driven by the κ-mechanism (opacity-gravity cycling in stellar envelopes). Surface brightness fluctuation distances require stellar population models governed by gravitational stellar evolution.
items.what_breaks: The dome reinterprets distance-redshift as aetheric refraction but the distance measurements themselves come from gravitationally-calibrated standard candles. The calibration chain is: Cepheid pulsation (gravity) → distance ladder → Hubble flow.

items.dependency: The dome's solar formula uses 23.45° obliquity — Earth's axial tilt, which is a gravitational parameter (maintained by gyroscopic precession against solar/lunar tidal torques).
items.what_breaks: The 23.45° value is borrowed from globe orbital mechanics. On a flat disc, there is no axis to tilt and no mechanism for seasonal variation without importing this gravitational parameter.

items.dependency: Same as WIN-001/002/029/038. Schumann cavity requires gravity-supported atmosphere.
items.what_breaks: G3 storm effects on Schumann resonance depend on ionospheric conductivity changes in a gravity-stratified atmosphere.

items.dependency: P-wave propagation through Earth's interior follows Snell's law through a gravitationally-stratified density profile. The shadow zone at 104°-140° arises from the liquid outer core, whose existence and depth are determined by gravitational differentiation.
items.what_breaks: On a flat disc, there is no spherical density stratification, no core-mantle boundary at 2,891 km depth, and no mechanism for P-wave refraction producing a 104° shadow. The dome claims this as geometric evidence but has no seismic velocity model.

items.dependency: Hemispheric heat distribution depends on atmospheric and oceanic circulation, both driven by pressure gradients in a gravity-supported fluid envelope. The thermohaline circulation is density-driven (gravitational buoyancy).
items.what_breaks: Without gravity, there are no convection cells, no density-driven ocean currents, and no mechanism for differential heat transport between hemispheres.

items.dependency: Claims to explain GRACE satellite gravity data showing a mass deficit under East Antarctica. GRACE measures gravitational acceleration from orbit using satellite-to-satellite ranging — the entire measurement chain is gravitational (satellite orbits, ranging, gravity field inversion).
items.what_breaks: GRACE satellites are in gravitational orbits at ~500 km altitude. The dome has no satellite orbit model (OPEN-007). The 'gravity hole' interpretation requires a gravitational framework the dome doesn't possess. The dome's own g(r) formula drops ~90% at the Antarctic rim, contradicting GRACE measurements.

tidal_cluster.note: All 5 tidal WINs are GRAVITY-DIRECT. The dome adopts tidal constituent periods without any tidal force derivation. OPEN-003 concedes no lunar mechanics. The dome's local moon produces wrong tidal geometry (one spike vs two bulges).

eclipse_gravity_cluster.note: Eclipse WINs depend on both orbital mechanics (eclipse prediction) and gravitational measurements (gravimeters). The dome borrows eclipse timing from globe ephemeris and reads globe-calibrated instruments. E-PRED-C (0.0 μGal) contradicts WIN-012 (κ requires non-zero Δg).

schumann_cluster.note: All 5 Schumann WINs are GRAVITY-INDIRECT — the EM cavity requires a gravity-supported atmosphere. Without gravitational atmospheric stratification, there is no ionosphere at ~85 km and no cavity to resonate.

cosmological_cluster.note: All 4 cosmological WINs depend on gravitational physics (cosmic expansion, CMB potential wells, cluster dynamics, Cepheid pulsation). The dome has no cosmological framework — no expansion, no structure formation, no gravity at extragalactic scales.

orbital_mechanics_cluster.note: 7 WINs depend on Earth's or Moon's gravitational orbit. The dome imports orbital parameters (obliquity, eccentricity, nodal period) without deriving them from disc geometry. OPEN-001 and OPEN-003 concede the dependency.

cross_cutting_analysis.the_structural_argument: The dome model has zero gravitational equations. It never derives g, never specifies a mass or density, never explains why objects fall toward the disc surface. Yet 14 of its WINs directly measure gravitational quantities, and 19 more depend on gravity-sustained phenomena. The g(r) formula in monitor.py (confirmed EXP-025) is WGS84 Somigliana — it IS globe gravity with dome coordinate labels. If you removed all gravitational inputs from the dome model (tidal periods, orbital parameters, gravimeter readings, satellite data, atmospheric structure), 33 of 67 WINs — nearly half the scoreboard — would have no observations to claim as predictions.
